title: Handle SIGPIPE to prevent machine stuck in Starting state

comments.body: I added a test with the help of AI, needs more eyes on it
@jiridostal thank you for your submission.  if you push again, could you summarize things a just a tad in your commit message.

> @jiridostal thank you for your submission. if you push again, could you summarize things a just a tad in your commit message.

Does the team have some sort of AI policy? In other words, should there be something in the commit message saying that it was written with AI, kind of like a "Signed-off" tag?

> Does the team have some sort of AI policy? In other words, should there be something in the commit message saying that it was written with AI, kind of like a "Signed-off" tag?

Our policy is that if you do a DCO signoff (which our project requires), you are vouching for the code whether your buddy helped you or AI did.  You sign your name,  you vouch for it and the ramifications therein.

The initial signal handling implemented in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25832 did not have any tests, otherwise I would get inspired (I'm no GO person :-D). Thanks for the comments, I'll try addressing these

Given the comment by @Luap99 I'm currently unsure if I'm able to provide good enough testing approach to this. This issue has been reported in Podman Desktop multiple times and it would be great to get to a conclusion, especially when the CI testing takes significantly longer than the actual fix.

Certainly, I believe a flaky test is worse than having no test. I was not aware of how complex it is to test signals. 

Can I add a `No New Test` label? @Luap99

> Certainly, I believe a flaky test is worse than having no test. I was not aware of how complex it is to test signals.
> 
> Can I add a `No New Test` label? @Luap99

I added the label, I rather merge this without the test.
